description: Phase 3 — Backend: Beach Profiles + API Skeleton. Seed MongoDB with 8 curated Greek beach profiles, create Mongoose models, and expose GET /beaches, GET /beaches/:id, and GET /beaches/:id/forecast endpoints with stub forecast data.

userStories.title: beach_profiles.json
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the curated beach profiles stored as a JSON file in the backend so that the seed script and any future tooling can read them without hitting the database.
acceptanceCriteria: backend/src/data/beach_profiles.json created containing a JSON array of exactly 8 beach objects
The 8 beaches in order: vouliagmeni-athens, mesachti-ikaria, langouvardos-filiatra, falasarna-crete, palaiohora-crete, agios-georgios-naxos, kokkino-limanaki-rafina, kolimpithra-tinos

Each beach object matches the schema in docs/beach-profiles.md exactly — field names, types, and values copied verbatim from the profiles documented there (including windScoringLogic blocks for wave-generating-onshore beaches)
Langouvardos weights: { swellDirection: 0.35, swellPeriod: 0.25, swellHeight: 0.20, wind: 0.15, tide: 0.05 } — standard open beach break defaults for beaches without explicit weights in docs
All other beaches without explicit weights use: { swellDirection: 0.20, swellPeriod: 0.20, swellHeight: 0.25, wind: 0.30, tide: 0.05 } (wave-generating-onshore defaults)
The file is valid JSON parseable with JSON.parse
Typecheck passes (npm run typecheck)
userStories.notes: Copy field values verbatim from docs/beach-profiles.md. Swell direction ranges that cross 360° (e.g. [340, 20]) are intentional — do not alter them. windScoringLogic is present only on wave-generating-onshore beaches.

userStories.title: Mongoose models — Beach and ForecastSnapshot
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Mongoose schemas and TypeScript interfaces for Beach and ForecastSnapshot so that all database operations are type-safe.
acceptanceCriteria: backend/src/models/Beach.ts created, exporting IBeach TypeScript interface, BeachSchema Mongoose schema, and Beach Mongoose model (mongoose.model<IBeach>('Beach', BeachSchema))

IBeach interface includes: id (string), name (string), coords ({lat, lon}), offshoreCoords? ({lat, lon}), region (string), description (string), shorelineNormalDeg (number), idealSwellDirection ([number,number]), acceptableSwellDirection ([number,number]), minSwellHeightM (number), idealSwellHeightM ([number,number]), minSwellPeriodS (number), idealSwellPeriodS ([number,number]), maxOnshoreWindKmh (number), idealWindDescription (union), windScoringLogic? (Record<string,unknown>), weights ({swellDirection,swellPeriod,swellHeight,wind,tide}: all number), sheltered (boolean), longPeriodSwellRefracts (boolean), skillLevel (union), tags (string[]), notes? (string)
BeachSchema uses id (string, required, unique) as the primary business key
backend/src/models/ForecastSnapshot.ts created, exporting IHourlyForecast, IDailySummary, IForecastSnapshot interfaces, ForecastSnapshotSchema, and ForecastSnapshot Mongoose model
IForecastSnapshot: beachId (string), fetchedAt (Date), hourlyForecasts (IHourlyForecast[]), dailySummaries (IDailySummary[])
IHourlyForecast: timestamp (Date), rawData (Record<string,unknown>), surfScore (number), label ('poor'|'maybe'|'good'|'very-good'), reasons (string[])
IDailySummary: date (string), bestWindowStart (string), bestWindowEnd (string), peakScore (number), overallLabel ('poor'|'maybe'|'good'|'very-good')
ForecastSnapshotSchema indexes beachId (for lookup) and fetchedAt descending (for latest-first queries)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Do not import from index.ts in model files (no circular dependencies). Use mongoose.model() not mongoose.Model. The id field is the string business key — Mongoose adds _id automatically.

userStories.title: Seed script — upsert beaches and stub forecasts
userStories.description: As a developer, I want an npm run seed script that populates MongoDB with all 8 beach profiles and one stub ForecastSnapshot per beach so that the API returns meaningful data immediately after setup.
acceptanceCriteria: backend/src/scripts/seed.ts created
Script reads backend/src/data/beach_profiles.json and upserts all 8 beaches using Beach.findOneAndUpdate({ id: beach.id }, beach, { upsert: true, new: true, setDefaultsOnInsert: true })

Script creates one stub ForecastSnapshot per beach using these deterministic scores — vouliagmeni-athens: 42/maybe, mesachti-ikaria: 71/good, langouvardos-filiatra: 58/maybe, falasarna-crete: 83/very-good, palaiohora-crete: 35/poor, agios-georgios-naxos: 61/good, kokkino-limanaki-rafina: 48/maybe, kolimpithra-tinos: 77/good

Each stub ForecastSnapshot has fetchedAt set to new Date(), one hourlyForecast entry at that timestamp with rawData: {}, reasons: ['Stub data — real forecast available after Phase 4'], and one dailySummary for today's ISO date string
Snapshot creation is idempotent: skip if a ForecastSnapshot for that beachId already exists (use findOne check before insert)
Script logs 'Seeded N beaches' and 'Created M snapshots (K already existed)' to stdout
Script calls connectDB() from backend/src/db/connection.ts before operations and calls process.exit(0) after completion
'seed': 'tsx src/scripts/seed.ts' added to backend/package.json scripts
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The seed script connects to MongoDB using MONGO_URI from process.env — requires the .env file or Docker environment. It must call process.exit(0) explicitly or the tsx process will hang waiting for the Mongoose connection to close.

userStories.title: GET /beaches endpoint
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a GET /beaches endpoint that returns all beaches with their current surf score so that the frontend beach listing has data to display.
acceptanceCriteria: backend/src/routes/beaches.ts created with an Express Router

GET /beaches implemented: fetches all beaches from MongoDB, for each beach finds the latest ForecastSnapshot (sort fetchedAt: -1, limit 1), assembles response with fields: id, name, region, coords, skillLevel, tags, currentScore (first hourlyForecast surfScore or null), currentLabel (first hourlyForecast label or null), lastUpdated (snapshot fetchedAt or null)
Response array is sorted by currentScore descending — beaches with null score sort to the end
Router mounted in backend/src/index.ts: app.use('/', beachesRouter) — full endpoint path is GET /beaches
curl http://localhost:3000/beaches returns HTTP 200 with Content-Type: application/json and an array of 8 objects after npm run seed
Response omits MongoDB _id and __v fields
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use Promise.all() to fetch latest snapshots for all beaches in parallel rather than sequentially. Omit _id/__v using .lean() and manual field selection or .select('-_id -__v').

userStories.title: GET /beaches/:id endpoint
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a GET /beaches/:id endpoint that returns a single beach's full profile plus current score so that the frontend detail view has all the data it needs.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /beaches/:id implemented in backend/src/routes/beaches.ts
Returns the full beach document (all profile fields from IBeach) plus currentScore, currentLabel, lastUpdated from the latest ForecastSnapshot
Returns HTTP 404 { error: 'Beach not found' } if :id does not match any beach in the beaches collection
Response omits _id and __v fields
curl http://localhost:3000/beaches/mesachti-ikaria returns HTTP 200 with the full Mesachti profile including currentScore: 71 and currentLabel: 'good'
curl http://localhost:3000/beaches/nonexistent-id returns HTTP 404 with { error: 'Beach not found' }
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: GET /beaches/:id/forecast endpoint
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a GET /beaches/:id/forecast endpoint that returns the latest ForecastSnapshot for a beach so that the frontend can display forecast data.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /beaches/:id/forecast implemented in backend/src/routes/beaches.ts
First checks if the beach exists in the beaches collection — returns HTTP 404 { error: 'Beach not found' } if not
Fetches the latest ForecastSnapshot for the beachId (sort fetchedAt: -1, limit 1) — returns HTTP 404 { error: 'No forecast data for this beach' } if none exists
Returns the ForecastSnapshot document with _id and __v omitted
curl http://localhost:3000/beaches/mesachti-ikaria/forecast returns HTTP 200 with a ForecastSnapshot containing one hourlyForecast entry and one dailySummary
curl http://localhost:3000/beaches/nonexistent-id/forecast returns HTTP 404 with { error: 'Beach not found' }
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Check beach existence before querying snapshots so the error message is accurate. All three endpoints share the same beaches.ts router file.
